last_item: Band-1: secrets-at-rest — log-scrubbing half (S3 creds / Pushover token / admin hash + session secret never logged)
last_result: done — DB file 0600/dir 0700 + mode test already existed, so this closed the 'never logged' half. Gave every secret-bearing struct a redacting fmt.Stringer + slog.LogValuer + fmt.GoStringer via new core.Redact/core.RedactBytes (fixed REDACTED marker, leaks nothing incl. length): config.S3Target (SecretKey, CipherPass), store.AuthRecord (Argon2id hash + raw HMAC SessionSecret), server.alertChannelConfig (PushoverToken, WebhookURL). Now no log line/error/fmt verb (%v/%+v/%s/%#v, incl. nested-in-parent %+v) leaks them. Non-secret ids kept visible (S3 AccessKey json:access_key; PushoverUser kept by maskAlertChannels). Per-struct log-scrubbing tests assert secrets absent + marker present across text+JSON slog handlers and all fmt verbs; JSON API unaffected (json:"-" stands). Reviewed; closed the %#v GoStringer gap the reviewer flagged.
notes: Remaining band-1: login brute-force lockout (rate-limit/lockout after N fails — note AuthRecord already has FailedAttempts + LockedUntil columns and SetPasswordHash resets them, so the store half may be partly built; verify throttle is end-to-end tested via the login handler/authenticator). Then band 1.5 (data durability: last-good-backup surfacing, loud alert on stale/failed backup, S3-as-default nudge, restore-test surfacing). Secret-redaction pattern: see learnings — Stringer+LogValuer+GoStringer, core.Redact, structs config.S3Target/store.AuthRecord/server.alertChannelConfig. Integration tests are `//go:build integration`, skip unless INDIEPG_TEST_SOCKET set, NOT in normal gate. go/psql/pg_ctl are snap-sandboxed (run with sandbox disabled). TMPDIR is empty in this shell — use /tmp explicitly.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
